variable_identity.standardized_name_en: arterial blood-gas carbon dioxide partial pressure event

immutable_core.semantic_intent: arterial blood-gas carbon dioxide partial pressure observation
immutable_core.semantic_definition: A time-stamped arterial blood-gas carbon dioxide partial-pressure measurement event. The variable is event-level and must not be reused for baseline, summary, score, or stay-level aggregate outputs.
immutable_core.same_name_cross_database_rule: Any database asset published as std_paco2 must preserve this arterial blood-gas carbon dioxide partial pressure observation meaning.

canonical_representation.range_role_note: This is the current cleaned retained-output range for the governed opening build, not a claim that every raw source row in every database is already valid.

external_alignment.alignment_policy: Use external terminologies as semantic anchors when reliable, but do not make them the backbone of the standard-variable system.

new_variable_trigger_examples: changing std_paco2 from an event-level measurement to a baseline or summary variable
changing std_paco2 to a different clinical concept or composite score
version_bump_trigger_examples: adding a new reviewed database mapping while preserving the same event-level meaning
locking a reviewed external semantic anchor without changing the variable meaning

allowed_database_specific_variation: source code systems and source codes
database-specific timing anchors and local structural keys
disallowed_same_name_variation: publishing a baseline or stay-level aggregate under the same variable id
publishing a neighboring but clinically distinct measurement under the same variable id

execution_governance.formal_output_rule: Formal std_paco2 MVP execution should go through execution.py reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec.
execution_governance.future_non_bypassable_rule: Formal std_paco2 outputs should be generated only through the governed execution.py path.
